response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong web and full-stack credentials, but the role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise for industrial automation platforms. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (~6 months, a decade ago) and a bachelor thesis project; his systems-level performance work was primarily in DLang. He shows no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial-protocol experience, making him a weak fit despite his overall seniority.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software experience spanning web, embedded, and low-latency systems
Proven high-performance, concurrent programming background (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec in DLang)
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience — brief and dated (2014–2015), with recent work in TypeScript/Node.js
No demonstrated industrial cybersecurity, SDL, or IEC 62443 exposure required by the role
No MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA), or automation-machinery domain background
